title: Save over $100 on this feature-rich Asus AM5 motherboard with Wi-Fi 7, USB4 & DDR5-8000 support TUF Gaming X870-Plus is on sale for just $170
summary: The Asus TUF Gaming X870-Plus motherboard is on sale for $170, offering Wi-Fi 7, USB4, and DDR5-8000 support at a discounted price. This is a significant $110 discount from its original price of $280. For its price, it provides exceptional performance, features, and reliability.
insight: High-performance motherboard now affordable for budget-conscious buyers.

title: Tennessee grandmother wrongly jailed for six months, latest victim of AI-driven misidentification facial recognition is jailing the wrong people, but police keep using it anyway
summary: A Tennessee grandmother was wrongly jailed for 6 months due to AI-driven facial recognition misidentification. The incident highlights the flawed reliability of facial recognition technology. She was later identified as an innocent woman.
insight: Facial recognition software can falsely incriminate innocent people with alarming frequency.

title: Microsoft s unhackable Xbox One has been hacked by 'Bliss' the 2013 console finally fell to voltage glitching, allowing the loading of unsigned code at every level
summary: A hack dubbed 'Bliss' has successfully breached the Xbox One's security, exploiting a voltage glitch to load unsigned code at every level. This achievement marks a significant milestone in the gaming console's hacking history. The breakthrough was unveiled at the RE//verse 2026 conference.
insight: Security breaches have significant implications for gaming console ecosystems

title: AMD's upcoming RDNA 5 GPUs might improve dual-issue execution & use shader units more efficiently LLVM patch adds new FMA instruction to ease compiling
summary: A new LLVM patch has added improved instructions for RDNA 5 GPUs, enabling compilers to utilize dual-issue execution more efficiently. This change introduces the V_FMA_F32 and VOPD3 instruction formats, easing compilation and potentially increasing FP32 throughput in certain workloads. The patch aims to improve the performance and efficiency of AMD's upcoming RDNA 5 GPUs.
insight: Unlocking dual-issue execution for increased FP32 throughput performance

title: ASML workers still in the dark seven weeks after 1,700 management cuts announced cuts represent 4% of its global workforce
summary: ASML workers remain uncertain 7 weeks after 1,700 management cuts announcement
insight: Uncertainty fuels unrest among remaining employees

title: 385TB video game archive saved by fans Myrient has been '100% backed up' and validated, torrents being generated
summary: A 385TB video game archive has been 100% backed up by fans through the Save Myrient community, with torrents now being generated for distribution.
insight: Preservation of massive gaming library ensures cultural and historical gaming content

title: Chinese GPU-maker Lisuan flaunts new design details for its LX 7G100 gaming card - also updates LX GPU product pages with server and workstation specs
summary: Lisuan has unveiled the LX 7G100 gaming card with new design details and updated product pages for the LX GPU series, which now encompasses all previous 7G105 models.
insight: Unified branding simplifies customer GPU purchasing experience greatly.

title: Apple's MacBook Neo modded to a 1 TB SSD, breaking the firm's 512 GB barrier base 256 GB model gets modded in expert NAND swap surgery
summary: DirectorFeng, a Chinese expert technician, successfully modified a MacBook Neo logic board to a 1 TB SSD, breaking Apple's 512 GB barrier. This mod was achieved through an expert NAND chip swap surgery. The result is the world's only MacBook Neo with a terabyte of storage.
insight: Technicians can bypass Apple's storage limitations through expert hardware modifications

title: Elon Musk says his chipmaking 'Terafab Project' venture will launch in seven days Musk's latest moonshot multi-billion project launches on a Saturday
summary: Elon Musk's 'Terafab Project' semiconductor production venture is set to launch in seven days.
insight: Potential disruption to global chip market with new production capacity

title: ASRock launches new Frankensteined motherboard with one DDR4 slot and two DDR5 slots Intel board signals the RAM apocalypse is truly nigh
summary: ASRock's H610M Combo II motherboard supports both DDR4 and DDR5 RAM, addressing current shortages. It's a barebones option lacking PCIe 4.0 storage, but it can help users build PCs during this period. This board caters to those who need to upgrade their systems now.
insight: Adaptable motherboards help users circumvent RAM shortages and upgrades.

title: Sega s missing link Saturn 'TRIP accelerator' project was real 1996-era plans revealed by engineer for the first time
summary: An ex-engineer revealed plans for the 'TRIP' Saturn graphics accelerator, a project developed in 1996. The proposed accelerator was based on Hitachi's SH-3 processor. It aimed to boost Saturn's performance.
insight: Hidden history of a potential Saturn performance upgrade revealed

title: Microscopes can clearly see the video on the surface of a CED 'LaserDisc,' discovers Techtuber a 12-inch vinyl-like disc that stores SD analog video
summary: Researchers using a microscope can clearly view images on a CED LaserDisc's surface, revealing details of stored analog video.
insight: Stored analog video is physically etched onto the disc's surface.

title: Clean-room reimplementation of DR-DOS hits early beta, modernizing the operating system 38 years after its debut runs Doom, Warcraft, SimCity, and other period-appropriate titles
summary: DR-DOS version 9.0 has reached early beta with Revision 291 available for download, featuring modernization and compatibility with classic games.
insight: Reviving a 38-year-old OS for modern gaming experiences.

title: 40-year-old Arcade classic shoot em up Gradius gets pure ASCII PC remake you can even save your gaming screenshots as .TXT files
summary: A developer has recreated the 1985 arcade classic Gradius in pure ASCII, allowing players to experience the game without graphics. The remake is available on PC and features a simple yet challenging gameplay experience. Players can also save their gaming screenshots as .TXT files.
insight: Retro gaming gets a new, text-based interpretation for accessibility.

title: SparkFun OpenScale IoT An ESP32 board with HX711 ADC for smart scales with WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity
summary: The SparkFun OpenScale IoT board offers an ESP32-based IoT smart scale solution with open-source hardware and firmware, featuring HX711 ADC, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth connectivity for precise weight data and live updates.
insight: Enabling easy, precise, and wireless smart scale data collection and monitoring.

title: M5Stamp C6LoRa tiny (18 15 2.3 mm) SMD module pairs ESP32-C6 with SX1262 LoRa chip
summary: The M5Stamp C6LoRa is a compact LoRa module combining ESP32-C6 with SX1262 for long-distance communication. It measures 18 15 2.3 mm, suitable for space-constrained systems. The module includes an LNA and I/O expander for improved reception performance.
insight: Ultra-compact LoRa module for space-constrained, long-range wireless applications.

title: Pok mon Go's AR data has been turned into centimeter-accurate navigation for delivery robots
summary: Niantic Spatial's AI system commercializes Pokémon Go's AR data, creating a high-precision world model for precise device location. This visual positioning system can locate devices within a few centimeters. It enables applications in delivery robots and beyond.
insight: Pokémon Go's data is revolutionizing real-world navigation accuracy.

title: Gig workers are getting paid to film their daily chores to train robots
summary: Gig workers are being paid to film their daily chores to train robots, providing detailed real-world movement examples.
insight: Robot training relies on real-world movement examples, not just online data.

title: GPS is getting jammed in the Strait of Hormuz, and ships are appearing in circles
summary: GPS interference in the Strait of Hormuz is causing AIS system failures. This may lead to navigational errors among tankers carrying large amounts of oil. The narrow waterways make this a potentially catastrophic situation.
insight: Navigation errors in narrow waterways pose catastrophic oil spill risks.

title: HandBrake remains the go-to free encoder for compressing and converting video
summary: HandBrake is a free video tool that converts and compresses video files. It supports multiple platforms and widely compatible codecs. Users can convert almost any video format.
insight: Free and versatile video converter for cross-platform compatibility

title: This $155 ESP32-powered DIY drone can hit 67 mph
summary: A DIY drone, ESP-Blast, utilizes an ESP32 board as its flight controller, achieving speeds of up to 67 mph. This unconventional approach deviates from typical high-performance drones that rely on dedicated hardware. The ESP32's integration of Wi-Fi and Bluetooth adds connectivity to the drone.
insight: Repurposing versatile connected devices for high-speed applications

title: AMD unveils OpenClaw to run AI agents locally on Ryzen and Radeon hardware
summary: AMD introduces OpenClaw, a technology to run AI agents locally on Ryzen and Radeon hardware as part of their Agent Computer initiative, allowing users to control their data and AI computing environment.
insight: More local AI control reduces dependence on cloud services.

title: Testing AMD FSR 4.1: Sharper Images, But DLSS Still Leads
summary: AMD's FSR 4.1 update promises improved detail and reduced motion blur. We tested it against FSR 4 and Nvidia's DLSS 4.5. While FSR 4.1 shows improvement, DLSS still leads in image quality.
insight: FSR 4.1 offers sharper images, but DLSS remains superior
